text:
Serbians gathered in front of Belgrade City Hall on Monday evening for a homecoming celebration, hailing athletes who brought home five medals from the Olympic Games in Paris.

Novak Djokovic, Serbia’s most successful tennis player ever, won his first gold medal at the Olympics – another trophy to add to his huge collection. Djokovic also became the oldest tennis player ever to win an Olympic gold.

“I have had the honour and pleasure of being on this special [City Hall] balcony several times in my career, but each time you surprise and inspire me more and more,” Djokovic told the crowd. “This is the most beautiful feeling that an athlete from Serbia can experience.”

Serbia won three gold medals, one silver and one bronze in Paris – fewer than in Tokyo 2020, when the country’s athletes won nine medals.

Serbia’s water polo team won their third Olympic gold in a row, after previous triumphs in Rio de Janeiro and Tokyo, while Zorana Arunovic and Damir Mikec won gold in the air pistol mixed team event.

Serbian taekwondo athlete Aleksandra Perisic won a silver medal at her first Olympic Games.

The Serbian men’s basketball team took home bronze after losing to the eventual winners the United States in the semi-final and then defeating Germany to secure third position.

“Thank you for supporting us throughout the entire tournament, thank you for this tonight,” men’s basketball captain Bogdan Bogdanovic told well-wishers in Belgrade.

title: Serbians Hail Djokovic and Olympic Medal-Winners at Belgrade Homecoming | Balkan Insight
author: Katarina Baletic
description: Fans gathered in Belgrade to cheer tennis ace Novak Djokovic, who won his first Olympic gold, and to welcome home Serbia’s other medal-winners from Paris.
